Eigenvalue power ratio for an integral/series-defined 2x2 operator

A linear operator T on ℂ^2 is represented (in the standard basis) by the 2×2 matrix
            ⎡  ∫₀¹ 4x dx        -  \sum_{n=1}^∞ (1/2)^n ⎤
  M   =      ⎢                                            ⎥ .
            ⎣  \sum_{n=1}^∞ (1/2)^n     ∫₀¹ 4x dx           ⎦

Let λ be the eigenvalue of M with Im(λ)>0. Define Ω = λ^10 and
  Ψ = Re(Ω) / Im(Ω).

Write Ψ in lowest terms as p/q with p,q \in ℤ and q>0. Compute (p+q) mod 1000, as the unique integer in {0,1,...,999} congruent to p+q modulo 1000.

Solution.
Step 1 (constant_evaluation_for_matrix_entries)
Evaluate the constants:
- ∫₀¹ 4x dx = [2x^2]₀¹ = 2.
- \sum_{n=1}^∞ (1/2)^n is a geometric series with first term 1/2 and ratio 1/2, so it sums to (1/2)/(1-1/2)=1.

Hence
  M = ⎡2  -1⎤
      ⎣1   2⎦.

Step 2 (eigenvalue_from_characteristic_polynomial)
Compute the characteristic polynomial:
  det(M - tI) = det⎡2-t  -1 ⎤ = (2-t)^2 + 1.
               ⎣ 1   2-t⎦
So eigenvalues satisfy (2-t)^2 = -1, i.e.
  t = 2 ± i.
By the selection rule Im(λ)>0, we choose
  λ = 2 + i.

Step 3 (polar_form_power_and_ratio via exact complex powering)
Compute Ω = (2+i)^10 by repeated squaring (exact arithmetic):
  (2+i)^2 = 4 + 4i + i^2 = 3 + 4i.
  (2+i)^4 = (3+4i)^2 = 9 + 24i + 16i^2 = -7 + 24i.
  (2+i)^8 = (-7+24i)^2 = 49 - 336i + 576i^2 = -527 - 336i.

Then
  (2+i)^10 = (2+i)^8(2+i)^2
           = (-527-336i)(3+4i)
           = (-527 \cdot 3) + (-527 \cdot 4i) + (-336i \cdot 3) + (-336i \cdot 4i)
           = -1581 - 2108i - 1008i - 1344i^2
           = -1581 - 3116i + 1344
           = -237 - 3116i.

Thus
  Re(Ω) = -237,   Im(Ω) = -3116,
and
  Ψ = Re(Ω)/Im(Ω) = (-237)/(-3116) = 237/3116.

Step 4 (reduce_fraction_and_apply_modulus)
Check that 237/3116 is already in lowest terms:
  3116 = 237 \cdot 13 + 35
  237  = 35 \cdot 6 + 27
  35   = 27 \cdot 1 + 8
  27   = 8 \cdot 3 + 3
  8    = 3 \cdot 2 + 2
  3    = 2 \cdot 1 + 1
so gcd(237,3116)=1.

Therefore p=237, q=3116, and
  p+q = 237 + 3116 = 3353 \equiv 353 (mod 1000).

So the required output is 353.

Uniqueness Test
1) The matrix entries are uniquely determined by the convergent integral and series evaluations, so M is unique.

2) The characteristic polynomial has roots 2±i, a conjugate pair. Exactly one has positive imaginary part, so the selection rule Im(λ)>0 determines λ uniquely.

3) With λ fixed, Ω=λ^10 and Ψ=Re(Ω)/Im(Ω) are uniquely determined (and Im(Ω)=-3116\neq0, so Ψ is well-defined).

4) Writing Ψ=p/q in lowest terms is unique because dividing by gcd(p,q) gives a unique reduced fraction with q>0. Hence (p+q) mod 1000 is uniquely determined.

353
